questions.question: Yogachar was a part of which among the following?
questions.explanation: Buddhism was mainly divided into Hinayana and Mahayana sects. The Mahayana sect was further divided into two kinds and they were Madhyamika or Sunyavada whose founder was Nagarjuna whereas the other was Yogachar or Vijnananavada started by Maitreyanath

questions.question: In Buddhist culture, prayer halls were known as -
questions.explanation: The Buddhist architecture was mainly divided into three forms and they are: • Stupas: Relics of Buddha or some prominent Buddhist monks are preserved • Chaitya: Prayer hall • Vihara: Residence

questions.question: The 23ʳᵈ Tirthankar of Jainsim Parshwanath was a prince of -
options: Other than those given in options
questions.explanation: The 23ʳᵈ Tirthankar of Jainism was Parshwanath or Parasnath who was a prince of Benaras. He left the throne and lived a life of a hermit. He died at Parasnath Hill in Jharkhand

questions.question: The mother of Mahavira belonged to the dynasty of which among the following places?
questions.explanation: Mahavira was born in 540 BC in a village Kundgrama near Vaishali in Bihar. His father Siddhartha was the leader of the Jnathrika Kshatriya clan whereas her mother Trishala was the sister of the King of Vaishali, Chetaka. Apart from that, Mahavira was also related to Bimbisara, the ruler of Magadha, who married Chellana, the daughter of Chetaka

questions.question: Which among the following Jain Tirthankar is NOT correctly matched with its symbol?
questions.explanation: Jain Tirthankars and their chosen symbols: TirthankarSymbol Rishabhanatha (Adinatha)Bull AjitanathaElephant SambhavanathaHorse AbhinandananathaMonkey SumatinathaGoose PadmaprabhaPadma SuparshvanathaSwastika ChandraprabhaCrescent Moon Pushpadanta(Suvidhinatha)Crocodile or Makara ShitalanathaShrivatsa ShreyanasanathaRhinoceros VasupujyaBuffalo VimalanathaBoar AnantanathaPorcupine/Falcon DharmanathaVajra ShantinathaAntelope or deer KunthunathaGoat AranathaNandyavarta or fish MāllīnāthaKalasha MunisuvrataTortoise NaminathaBlue lotus NeminathaShankha ParshvanathaSnake MahaviraLion

questions.question: The famous battle of Alexander and Porus is known in history as -
options: Other than those given in options
questions.explanation: Alexander invaded India in order to extend his kingdom in Asia in the year 326 BC. He defeated Porus who did not surrender before him and the battle is known as the Battle of Jhelum or in Greek, it is known as the Battle of Hydaspes in 326 BC. After this, Alexander left India and died in Babylon in 323 BC at the age of 33 years

questions.question: The invasion of Darius I took place in the year -
options: Other than those given in options
questions.explanation: The Archemian ruler Darius I invaded India from the North Western part of the country in 518 BC and annexed Punjab, West of Indus and also the Sindh province. This area constituted the 20ᵗʰ Province of the Iranian empire. From this property, Iran received the most of the revenue among all the lands as this was the most fertile area in the dynasty

questions.question: Which among the following was NOT a republican state among the 16 Mahajanapadas?
questions.explanation: During the reign of the various monarchies in the 16 mahajanapadas as well as the republican governments, the states were mainly divided according to the authority of administration in these provinces and they were monarchical and non-monarchical ones. Among the monarchical ones, Anga, Magadha, Vatsa, Kosal, Chedi, Sursena, Matsa etc were the prominent ones whereas among the non-monarchical ones, the famous ones were Vajji, Malla, Kuru, Panchal, Kamboja, Shakya, Koliya, Moriya etc

questions.question: Gautama Buddha is regarded as the ___ avatar of Vishnu.
questions.explanation: The ten best known avatars of Vishnu are collectively known as the Dashavatara. Vishnu's avatars descend to empower the good and fight evil, thereby restoring Dharma. The avatars in order are Matsya, Kurma, Varaha, Narasimha, Vamana, Parashurama, Rama, Krishna, Buddha and Kalki.

questions.question: The reign of Ajatasatru was during the period of -
options: Other than those given in options
questions.explanation: Bimbisara was succeeded by his son Ajatasatru who killed his father and conquered the throne. He gained complete control of Kashi and broke the amicable relation with his maternal uncle Prasenjit, the king of Kashi. He also conquered Vajji and built a fort at Rajagriha on the banks of the river Ganges

questions.question: Which among the following is NOT correctly matched regarding the Indus valley civilization?
questions.explanation: Some of the provinces of Indus valley civilization and their present locations: • Harappa: Sahiwal, Punjab in Pakistan • Mohenjodoro: Larkana, Sindh province in Pakistan • Chanhudro: Nawabshah, Sindh • Lothal: Ahmedabad, Gujarat • Banawali: Fatehabad, Haryana • Kalibanga: Hanumangarh, Rajasthan • Dholavira: Kutchh, Gujarat

questions.question: The largest building of Indus Valley civilization was found in -
questions.explanation: The largest building of the Indus Valley civilization was the Great Bath of Mohenjodaro which is now situated in Larkana district of Sindh Province in Pakistan.

questions.question: Which among the following was invented by the Indus valley civilization people for the first time in the world?
questions.explanation: The main crops of Indus Valley civilization were wheat and barley whereas the instances of rice cultivation were mainly found in Lothal and Rangpur. Other important crops were dates, mustard, sesamum, cotton etc. Indus people were the first to invent cotton in the whole world

questions.question: Vrihadarantyaka was an example of -
questions.explanation: The Upanishadas were philosophical texts and they are generally known as the Vedantas since they used to appear at the end of the Vedas. The oldest among all the Upanishads was Vrihadaranyaka and the total number of Upanishads was 108

questions.question: The number of unique hymns in Sama Veda was -
options: Other than those given in options
questions.explanation: Sama Veda is mainly known as the book of chants. The number of total hymns was 1549 but all excluding 75 was taken from the Rig Veda itself. The Veda is important for Indian music. On the other hand, these hymns were recited by Udagiri

questions.question: Jaiminaya was a Bramhana associated with -
questions.explanation: Bramhanas explain the hymns of the Vedas and they are mainly written in prose form. Every veda has some or the other hymn associated with it and they are: • Rig Veda: Aitareya,Kaushitiki • Sama Veda: Panchvisha, Shadvinsh, Chhandogya, Jaiminaya • Yajur Veda: Shatapatha, Taittariya • Atharva Veda: Gopatha

questions.question: The earliest explanation of 4 Ashramas was found in -
options: Other than those given in options
questions.explanation: The reference to 4 ashramas began in the later Vedic Period and the first such mention can be traced in Jabala Upanishad. These are Bramhacharya, Grihastha, Vanprastha and Sanyasa. This was introduced to attain 4 Purusharthas

questions.question: Which among the following explains Paisacha Vivaha?
options: Marrying the daughter of a defeated king
Marrying a girl after seducing her
questions.explanation: There are various kinds of Hindu marriage as was mentioned during the Later Vedic Age and they are as follows: • Bramha Vivaha: Giving the girl to a man with dowry • Daiva Vivaha: Giving the girl to the priest in lieu of his fees • Arsha Vivaha: Giving the girl to a man after accepting a bride price • Prajapatya Vivaha: Giving the girl to a man without asking any bride price • Gandharva Vivaha: Love marriage • Asura Vivaha: Marriage with a purchased girl • Rakshasa Vivaha: Marrying the daughter of a defeated king

questions.question: Krishna Deva Raya belonged to -
options: Other than those given in options
questions.explanation: The Tuluva dynasty was founded by Vir Narsimha Rao in 1505 AD in Vijayanagar kingdom. After him, Krishna Deva Raya was made the king and he maintained friendly relations with the Portugese and also built Vijaya Mahal apart from Vithal Swami Temple, Hazara Ram Temple etc. He was known as Andhra Bhoja, Andhra Pitamaha etc

questions.question: The philosophy of Pushti Marg was propounded by -
options: Other than those given in options
questions.explanation: Bhakti Movement became popular in India during the 15ᵗʰ century and Vallabhacharya was a famous exponent of this movement. He was a member of the Krishna Bhakti cult of Vaishavism. He propounded the philosophy of Pushti Marg. His time period was from 1479 AD to 1531 AD

questions.question: Who was the author of the book Bhavartha Ramayana?
questions.explanation: Eknath was a Bhakti Saint from Maharashtra and he wrote a contemporary on the Ramayana which was known as the Bhavartha Ramayana. He also another commentary on the eleventh book of Bhagavtha Purana

questions.question: Who among the following is known as the earliest exponent of Vishitadvaita philosophy?
questions.explanation: Ramanuja (1017-1137 AD) was the Vaishnava saint from South India. He was the earliest exponent of Bhakti movement and also the Vishitadvaita philosophy. His famous disciple was Kabir who is known as a famous Bhakti saint himself

questions.question: Shaikh Bhauddin Zakariya is a Sufu saint known as associated with -
options: Other than those given in options
questions.explanation: Shaikh Bahauddin Zakariya (1182-1262 AD) was the founder of Surha Wardi order who founded the first leading Khanqah in India in Multan

questions.question: Who was the first to use gun powder in India in warfare?
questions.explanation: Babar was the founder of the Mughal dynasty in India in 1526 AD and he defeated Ibrahim Lodhi to establish this kingdom in India. He passed away in 1530 AD at Agra and he was the first to use gun powder and artillery in India in case of warfare. He wrote his autobiography Tuzuk-i-Babri

questions.question: Humayunnama is the autobiography of Humayun written by Gulbadan Begum. She is related to Humayun as -
questions.explanation: Humayun was the son of Babur and he ascended the throne in 1530 AD but he was defeated by Sher Shah in 1539 AD. After that, he left to Iran and lived in exile for 12 years. His sister Gulbadan Begum wrote his autobiography, Humayunnama. He built Din Panah at Delhi as his second capital

questions.question: GT Road was built by Sher Shah between -
questions.explanation: Sher Shah defeated Humayun and ascended on the throne in Delhi after the Battle of Chausa. He issued the coin called Rupia and fixed standard weights and measures over the empire. He also improved communication by building several highways. He built the road between Calcutta and Peshwar named as Grand Trunk Road

questions.question: Akbar relinquished Jaziya tax from his empire in the year -
questions.explanation: Jaziya tax was introduced during the reign of Sultannate years in Delhi against the Hindus. However, Akbar abolished the Jaziya tax during his reign in the year 1564 AD

questions.question: Which among the following was founded by Bhagat Singh?
questions.explanation: Some revolutionary organizations in India: • Vyayam Mandala: Chapekar brothers • Mitra Mela: Savarkar Brothers • Anushilan Samiti: Pramath Nath Mitra • Abhinava Bharata: Vikram Damodar Savarkar • Swadesh Bandhav Samiti: Ashwini Kumar Dutta • Bharat Mata Society: Ajit Singh and Sufi Amba Prasad • Hindustan Republican Association: SN Sanyal • Hindustan Socialist Republican Association: Chandrasekhar Azad

questions.question: The Home Rule Movement was inspired by -
options: Other than those given in options
questions.explanation: BG Tilak founded the Indian version of the Indian Home Rule League in April 1916 whereas Annie Besant started this movement in India in September 1916. Soon after, this became very popular in India and the branches of this league started all over India.

questions.question: The Lucknow Pact was signed in the year -
questions.explanation: The Lucknow Pact was signed in the year 1916 and it was an important step towards achieving the unity between the Hindus and the Muslims. This pact forced the British government to announce its future policy of giving more space to Indians in the government and at the same time, starting local self governance bodies in various provinces in the country.
